description:
You are a data-analyst who has received two small CSV files and must migrate them into a SQLite database, then produce a short validation report so another script can automatically confirm the migration was done correctly.

    1. Source CSV files  
       • /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv  
       • /home/user/data/raw/orders.csv  

       Their exact, complete contents are:

       ── /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv ──  
       id,name,email  
       1,Alice Smith,alice@example.com  
       2,Bob Jones,bob@example.com  
       3,Charlie Chen,charlie@example.com  
       4,Diana Prince,diana@example.com  
       5,Evan Davis,evan@example.com  

       ── /home/user/data/raw/orders.csv ──  
       id,customer_id,product,quantity  
       1001,1,Keyboard,2  
       1002,1,Mouse,1  
       1003,2,Laptop,1  
       1004,3,Monitor,2  
       1005,3,USB Cable,5  
       1006,4,Headset,1  
       1007,4,Mouse Pad,3  
       1008,5,Webcam,1  

    2. Target database  
       • Create directory  /home/user/work/migration   (if it does not already exist).  
       • Inside that directory create a SQLite database file named  sales.db  containing TWO tables:  
         a) customers( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL )  
         b) orders( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, product TEXT NOT NULL, quantity INTEGER,  
                    FOREIGN KEY(customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id) )  

    3. Migration requirements  
       • Import all rows from both CSV files into their respective tables.  
       • DO NOT modify, re-order, or drop any rows.  
       • All eight orders must keep the customer_id relationships intact.  

    4. Validation requirements  
       After loading the data, run SQL queries to obtain:  
       • total number of rows in customers table  
       • total number of rows in orders table  
       • number of “orphan” orders (orders whose customer_id does NOT appear in customers.id)  

       Write those numbers to a plain-text file named  
       /home/user/work/migration/validation_report.log  

       The file MUST contain exactly four lines, with **no leading or trailing spaces**, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF).  
       The precise format the grader will check is:

           CUSTOMERS_ROWS:<customers_row_count>
           ORDERS_ROWS:<orders_row_count>
           ORPHAN_ORDERS:<orphan_count>
           MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS

       Example of a correct report (showing the expected final numbers for this data set):

           CUSTOMERS_ROWS:5
           ORDERS_ROWS:8
           ORPHAN_ORDERS:0
           MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS

       Any deviation in spelling, punctuation, capitalization, extra spaces, or line order will cause automated verification to fail.

    5. Expected complexity  
       A knowledgeable person could accomplish the entire task in roughly 5-10 shell commands (mkdir, sqlite3, .mode csv / .import, and echo or printf to build the log file).

    6. Deliverables that must exist at the end of the session  
       • /home/user/work/migration/sales.db   — contains the two populated tables exactly as specified.  
       • /home/user/work/migration/validation_report.log   — matches the four-line format above.  

    Follow normal terminal best-practices (inspect directories, preview CSVs, test SQL queries, etc.).  
    Do not hard-code the numbers in the log; instead derive them from the database you built.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv  
       id,name,email
       1,Alice Smith,alice@example.com
       2,Bob Jones,bob@example.com
       3,Charlie Chen,charlie@example.com
       4,Diana Prince,diana@example.com
       5,Evan Davis,evan@example.com

    2. /home/user/data/raw/orders.csv  
       id,customer_id,product,quantity
       1001,1,Keyboard,2
       1002,1,Mouse,1
       1003,2,Laptop,1
       1004,3,Monitor,2
       1005,3,USB Cable,5
       1006,4,Headset,1
       1007,4,Mouse Pad,3
       1008,5,Webcam,1

    Ground-truth values the autograder will check AFTER the agent finishes:

    • A file must exist: /home/user/work/migration/sales.db  
      – It must contain table “customers” with exactly 5 rows (ids 1-5).  
      – It must contain table “orders” with exactly 8 rows (ids 1001-1008).  
      – Running  
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers;  
        must return 5.  
      – Running  
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders;  
        must return 8.  
      – Running  
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o
            WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM customers c WHERE c.id = o.customer_id);
        must return 0.

    • A file must exist: /home/user/work/migration/validation_report.log  
      Exact contents (with single LF after each line) must be:

        CUSTOMERS_ROWS:5
        ORDERS_ROWS:8
        ORPHAN_ORDERS:0
        MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS

    The autograder will open the log file, compare line-by-line, and also verify the counts against the database queries above.
